canonicalChoiceReason: User designated as 'keep' slug; shorter, more general slug that covers all storage scenarios (home, vehicle, workplace) rather than home-only; published v1 in the 72-Hour Emergency Supplies parentTopic.
crossParentTopicNote: These guides are in different parentTopics (72-Hour Emergency Supplies vs Safe Drinking Water) and different layers (scenario vs preparedness). The merged guide retains the canonical guide's parentTopic/category/layer. The archive guide's richer content (detailed steps, whatNotToDo, backupAction, preparednessTips) is merged in. The archive guide's parentTopic (Safe Drinking Water) already has extensive coverage from other guides.

fieldDecisions.source: Kept 'Emergency Water Storage' — broader scope than 'Emergency Water Storage at Home'
Archive version is more specific with '1 gallon per person per day for 3 days' quantitative guidance
fieldDecisions.note: Synthesized to combine archive's specificity with canonical's broader scope

fieldDecisions.source: Archive version includes concrete quantity guidance
fieldDecisions.note: Synthesized to include both quantity and scope

fieldDecisions.source: Combined canonical's multi-location scope with archive's timing emphasis
Archive is more specific about storage method (food-grade containers, cool/dark locations)
Only archive guide had a backupAction about reusing PET soda bottles
6 steps from archive + 1 unique from canonical, deduplicated to 7 steps
fieldDecisions.note: Archive had more thorough step-by-step; canonical added 'rotate or refresh' which was already covered by archive step 6

fieldDecisions.source: 2 from canonical + 2 from archive, deduplicated to 3 unique warnings
fieldDecisions.note: Canonical's 'containers must be food-grade' and archive's 'chemicals can penetrate plastics' merged into one stronger warning

fieldDecisions.source: 3 items from archive; canonical had none
2 from canonical + 2 from archive, deduplicated to 3 unique red flags
fieldDecisions.note: Both had similar 'water smells/looks bad' entries — kept archive's more specific phrasing

fieldDecisions.source: 1 from canonical + 3 from archive, deduplicated to 4 tips
3 refs from canonical (all real URLs) + 2 from archive (real URLs, same CDC page); deduplicated to 3 unique references
fieldDecisions.note: Archive's two refs both pointed to the same CDC page already in canonical's refs

fieldDecisions.source: Union of both tag sets: water, storage, preparedness, home, rotation, cdc

conflicts.valueA: Preparedness / 72-Hour Emergency Supplies and Go-Bags / scenario
conflicts.valueB: Water Food / Safe Drinking Water in Emergencies / preparedness
conflicts.resolution: Using canonical guide's identity (Preparedness / 72-Hour Emergency Supplies / scenario) per user instruction. The archive guide's parentTopic already has robust coverage from purification and hydration guides.

Upgraded to preparedness_guide — the merged content is fundamentally about advance preparation (calculating supply, choosing containers, rotation schedule), which aligns with the preparedness guide type better than scenario.

Upgraded to preparedness — the merged guide is about advance planning, not in-the-moment action. This also avoids duplicating the scenario layer in the 72-Hour Supplies parentTopic which already has scenario guides.

archiveCandidateWarnings: emergency-water-storage-at-home has response_role 'reference' in the Safe Drinking Water parentTopic — archiving removes it from that cluster. However, water storage is tangential to that cluster's core purpose (water purification/sourcing), so impact is minimal.
No other guides have alternativeToGuideSlugs pointing to emergency-water-storage-at-home — safe to archive.
